title: Initialize a Repository & Make Your First Commit
objectives: Understand what version control is and why it matters
Initialize a new Git repository with git init
Create files, stage them with git add, and commit with git commit
hints: git init creates a hidden .git folder — that's where Git stores the entire history of your project
git add <file> stages a file (marks it for the next commit). git add . stages everything
git commit -m 'Your message' saves a snapshot. Write messages that explain WHY, not just what
assessmentCriteria: Student can explain the difference between a working directory and a repository
A new Git repository is initialized successfully
At least one file is created, staged, and committed
Commit message is descriptive and meaningful
